evidence_snippets: Use native controls only on both platforms
coverage_entries.reason: Sources mandate native device UI conventions.
Documents require standard menus and buttons for navigation.
Stakeholders repeatedly request a professional appearance.

evidence_snippets: keep our blue, gray, font sizes consistent
coverage_entries.reason: Sources call for consistent colors and font mapping.
Team states goal that agents require no training.
Requirement for intuitive, self-explanatory flows is explicit.

evidence_snippets: New hire should pick up in minutes
coverage_entries.reason: Notes specify minimal learning curve and quick pickup.
Text requires realtor-understandable labels and terminology.
Performance target for acceptable search timing is stated.
Explicit 30-second maximum search response commitment.
Sources require in-field CMA generation capability.
Multiple notes specify CMA under ~60 seconds.
Documents mandate syncing contacts and appointments.
Hourly pulls from office system are explicitly required.
Stakeholders demand 24/7 availability for agents.
Specifies 99.9% (999/1000) availability target.
Sources require usable offline mode with caching.

evidence_snippets: View cached searches, previous CMA reports
coverage_entries.reason: Documents state cached searches, CMAs, appointments viewable.
Sources require retaining prefs across crashes/outages.

evidence_snippets: On restart, 100% of saved prefs restored exactly.
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes explicitly demand full preference restoration.
Notes explicitly require multi-remote-user support.
Meeting and email specify 1000 concurrent users target.

evidence_snippets: Plan for growth to 5x (~5000) within a year.
coverage_entries.reason: Documents state 5x growth to ~5000 within a year.
Multiple artifacts require self-install without printed materials.
Goal explicitly sets 90%+ untrained success for install.

evidence_snippets: Target platforms: Windows CE and Palm OS only
coverage_entries.reason: Sources confirm v1 targets Windows CE and Palm OS.

evidence_snippets: Access limited to registered, licensed agents only.
coverage_entries.reason: Security notes and emails restrict access to licensed agents.
Documents call for authentication and authorization for users.

evidence_snippets: Input validation everywhere to avoid bad data
coverage_entries.reason: Requirements demand input validation to prevent bad data.

evidence_snippets: MLS query from device — must support typical criteria.
coverage_entries.reason: Notes require MLS queries from device with realtor-friendly criteria.
Map tap provides directions.
Hourly sync pulls contacts and appointments.

evidence_snippets: Show clear property images in results
coverage_entries.reason: Agents insist on clear photos in results.
Device listing create/update expressly requested.

evidence_snippets: Generate CMA in field. Quick comps
coverage_entries.reason: CMA generation in field with comp selection.
App will initiate calls to schedule appointments.

evidence_snippets: notify user of new or changed appointments
coverage_entries.reason: Device notifies realtor after sync completes.
Map view with property pins is required.
Notifications for client responses are specified.
Users explicitly request saving searches for later.

unsupported_implied.text: The system shall detect network interruptions during listing create/update and implement retry and deduplication logic so that partially-submitted updates do not create duplicate listings when the network is restored.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: Edge cases: network drops during update → retry later; do not create duplicates. Owen to define.
unsupported_implied.reason: The source explicitly requires behavior to handle interrupted listing submissions (retry and prevent duplicates). The gold set requires create/update listings and input validation but does not specify retry/deduplication semantics for network failures.

unsupported_implied.text: The system shall record and expose performance telemetry (e.g., time from search/CMA criteria submission to first result/render) and support monitoring of top-of-hour load/availability spikes.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: Owen: log the time from criteria entry to report. QA: We’ll run pass/fail around the search <= 30s metric and CMA <= 60s metric... Load test plan: 1000 simultaneous users around top of hour; measure page times + errors. Will add a high-water test at 5x to see bottlenecks.
unsupported_implied.reason: Multiple artifacts call for logging and load/monitoring (timing logs, QA load tests, monitoring top-of-hour spikes). The gold requirements specify performance targets and concurrency numbers but do not require telemetry, logging, or monitoring capabilities.

unsupported_implied.text: The user interface shall display the last-synced timestamp and surface a concise summary of changes (for example, '2 new showings added since 11:00') after each hourly sync.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: We will show a last-synced time and surface a note for new appointments after each run. ... After sync completes, device should notify user of new or changed appointments. Show last synced time. Example: “2 new showings added since 11:00”.
unsupported_implied.reason: The source explicitly requires showing last-synced time and a digest of changes post-sync. While the gold set requires notification of new appointments after synchronization, it does not require displaying the last-synced timestamp or a summarized list of what changed.
